findings.description: ZH definition contains katakana script (katakana tokens: sekkyua, buuto). JP katakana should not appear in ZH output; use Chinese characters or Latin equivalents.
findings.evidence: ZH def contains: katakana_matches=['セキュア','ブート']
findings.suggested_fix: Replace katakana tokens with Latin 'Secure' and 'Boot'; replace JP brackets with Chinese quotation marks.

findings.description: JP-style brackets used in ZH definition. Chinese convention uses standard Chinese quotation marks.
findings.evidence: occurrences: ['「セキュア」', '「安全」', '「ブート」', '「启动」', '「安全的计算机启动」']
findings.suggested_fix: Replace JP brackets with Chinese double quotation marks in ZH definitions.

findings.description: JP-style brackets used in ZH definition. Chinese convention uses standard Chinese quotation marks.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace JP brackets with Chinese double quotation marks in ZH definitions.

findings.description: JP-style brackets used in ZH definition. Chinese convention uses standard Chinese quotation marks.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace JP brackets with Chinese double quotation marks in ZH definitions.

findings.description: JP surface 'betone-reshon tesuto' (BE-ne-to-re-e-shon) is a typo for 'penetore-shon tesuto' (PE-ne-to-re-e-shon). BE (voiced dakuten) vs PE (semi-voiced handakuten) error. The typo propagates into the JP definition body.
findings.evidence: surface.jp='ベネトレーションテスト'; correct form is 'ペネトレーションテスト'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'ベネトレーション' with 'ペネトレーション' in surface.jp and throughout definition.jp.

findings.description: EN definition glosses 'Secure' as 'safe'. In English computing context, 'secure' means 'protected/hardened', not 'safe'. This conflates security and safety concepts for English learners.
Page 548 is a summary/review page that re-lists 13 terms already defined on pp542-547, producing expected duplicate JP surface terms across the chunk. Not an error if summary pages are intentional.
findings.evidence: 13 duplicate JP surfaces including DMZ, SSL/TLS, VPN, SSO, etc.
findings.suggested_fix: Add entity annotation is_summary=true or type='summary_term' to distinguish from primary definitions.

findings.description: 'ransomware-heno-taisaku' is a descriptive phrase ('countermeasures against ransomware'), not a canonical IT term. Using it as a term surface may confuse downstream glossary indexing.
findings.evidence: surface.jp='ランサムウェアへの対策'
findings.suggested_fix: Mark as type='section' or 'note', or rename surface to canonical term form.

summary: Chunk 53 (pp542-551, security/auth topic): 60 entities audited across 10 pages. 1 CRITICAL defect: JP surface typo 'ベネトレーション' should be 'ペネトレーション' (p544, voiced/semi-voiced dakuten error, propagates into definition body). 1 MAJOR defect: katakana script leak into ZH definition for セキュアブート (p546). 4 MINOR defects: JP brackets in ZH definitions (pp546/547/549), and 'Secure'='safe' conflation in EN (p546). 1 MINOR: descriptive phrase used as term surface (p548). 1 INFO: 13 expected duplicate terms on summary page 548. No U+FFFD, no trilingual parity gaps, no duplicate entity IDs. ZH surface glossary parity: 100% match on 28 of 33 terms (5 absent from glossary, all summary-page variants).
